steps.title: Mark the molecule contrast
steps.content: The first sentence says a flipped achiral molecule remains structurally identical. The second sentence shifts to a flipped chiral molecule, which becomes structurally inverted "rather than an identical one." The blank must mark a contrast between those two behaviors.

steps.title: Reject noncontrast transitions
steps.content: Choice A would signal restatement, but the second sentence does not repeat the first. Choice C would make the chiral molecule an example of the achiral molecule, and Choice D would signal similarity. The needed transition marks opposition between identical and inverted outcomes. The answer is B.
